title: Writing a problem-solution email
explanation:
Writing a problem-solution email

When writing about a problem, explain the situation clearly, describe the effect it has, and suggest a practical solution.

  • State the problem directly.
  • Give useful details.
  • Suggest one or two realistic solutions.
  • Use polite, constructive language.

questions.text: [*I am writing because several students are having problems with the timetable.|I write timetable problems many.|Problems timetable writing.]
[*As a result, some of us are arriving late for lessons.|As result some late lessons.|Result arrive late.]
[*A possible solution would be to move the first class twenty minutes later.|Possible solution move class twenty minutes later.|Solution class twenty later.]
[*I would appreciate it if you could consider this suggestion.|I appreciate consider suggestion.|Could consider suggestion appreciate.]

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A good problem-solution email should focus on practical ____.  | *solutions | platforms | weather
It is important to explain how the issue ____ people. | *affects | delays | publishes
The tone should remain polite and ____.  | *constructive | aggressive | careless
Try to make your suggestions sound ____.  | *realistic | impossible | dramatic
[*One option might be to provide clearer information in advance.|One option provide clearer information.|Provide clearer option in advance.]
[*This would help students organise their time more effectively.|This help students organise time effective.|Would help students effective.]
[*Please let me know if I can provide any further details.|Please let me know further details provide.|Further details if let me know.]
[*Thank you for taking the time to read this message.|Thank you read this message taking time.|Taking time read thank you.]
